clauses.title: Confidentiality Obligations
clauses.risk_reason: Strict confidentiality requirements
clauses.key_text: hold all Confidential Information of the other Party in strict confidence

clauses.title: Exclusions
clauses.risk_reason: Limited exceptions to confidentiality
clauses.key_text: Confidential Information shall not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available

clauses.title: Ownership
clauses.risk_reason: Exclusive ownership of confidential information
clauses.key_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the sole and exclusive property of the Disclosing Party

clauses.title: No License
clauses.risk_reason: No implied license to intellectual property
clauses.key_text: This Agreement does not grant any right, title, or interest in any intellectual property

clauses.title: Term and Termination
clauses.risk_reason: Automatic renewal and termination provisions
clauses.key_text: this Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one (1) year periods

clauses.title: Termination for Convenience
clauses.risk_reason: Termination with notice
clauses.key_text: Either Party may terminate upon thirty (30) days prior written notice

clauses.title: Effect of Termination
clauses.risk_reason: Obligations survive termination
clauses.key_text: Confidentiality obligations survive termination for five (5) years

clauses.title: Limitation of Liability
clauses.risk_reason: Limitation on indirect damages
clauses.key_text: IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, PUNITIVE, OR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES

clauses.title: Direct Damages Cap
clauses.risk_reason: Cap on direct damages
clauses.key_text: Each Party's total cumulative liability for any and all claims shall not exceed United States Dollars Fifty Thousand (USD 50,000)

clauses.title: Indemnification
clauses.risk_reason: Mutual indemnification obligations
clauses.key_text: Each Party shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the other Party from all losses, damages, liabilities, claims, and expenses

clauses.title: Fees
clauses.risk_reason: One-time access fee
clauses.key_text: Party B agrees to pay Party A a one-time access fee of United States Dollars Twenty-Five Thousand (USD 25,000)

clauses.title: Late Payment
clauses.risk_reason: Interest on late payments
clauses.key_text: Unpaid amounts shall accrue interest at one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month

clauses.title: Governing Law
clauses.risk_reason: Choice of law provision
clauses.key_text: This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of Delaware

clauses.title: Force Majeure Event
clauses.risk_reason: Excuse for non-performance due to force majeure
clauses.key_text: Neither Party shall be liable for failure or delay caused by circumstances beyond reasonable control

clauses.title: Automatic Renewal
clauses.risk_reason: Automatic renewal provision
clauses.key_text: this Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one (1) year periods

clauses.title: Confidentiality Obligations
clauses.risk_reason: The clause requires both parties to hold confidential information in strict confidence and protect it with reasonable care. The worst realistic outcome if invoked is a breach of confidentiality, which could lead to significant financial losses. This clause is standard in industry contracts, but the 5-year confidentiality term is relatively long.
clauses.key_text: 2.1 Non-Disclosure
steps: STEP 1: Each party must hold the other party's confidential information in strict confidence and protect it with reasonable care.
STEP 2: The worst realistic outcome if invoked is a breach of confidentiality, which could lead to significant financial losses.
STEP 3: This clause is standard in industry contracts, but the 5-year confidentiality term is relatively long.
STEP 4: Risk score: 60 (medium-high) due to the potential consequences of a breach of confidentiality.

clauses.title: Intellectual Property Ownership
clauses.risk_reason: The clause clearly states that all confidential information remains the sole and exclusive property of the disclosing party. The worst realistic outcome if invoked is a dispute over intellectual property ownership. This clause is standard in industry contracts.
clauses.key_text: 3.1 Ownership
steps: STEP 1: The disclosing party retains ownership of all confidential information.
STEP 2: The worst realistic outcome if invoked is a dispute over intellectual property ownership.
STEP 3: This clause is standard in industry contracts.
STEP 4: Risk score: 40 (medium) due to the potential for disputes over intellectual property ownership.

clauses.title: Term and Termination
clauses.risk_reason: The clause allows for termination with 30 days' notice, but also includes an automatic renewal provision. The worst realistic outcome if invoked is a sudden termination of the agreement, which could lead to financial losses. This clause is relatively standard in industry contracts.
clauses.key_text: 4.1 Term
steps: STEP 1: The agreement can be terminated with 30 days' notice.
STEP 2: The worst realistic outcome if invoked is a sudden termination of the agreement, which could lead to financial losses.
STEP 3: This clause is relatively standard in industry contracts, but the automatic renewal provision may be considered somewhat restrictive.
STEP 4: Risk score: 50 (medium) due to the potential consequences of sudden termination.

clauses.title: Limitation of Liability
clauses.risk_reason: The clause limits liability to $50,000, which may not be sufficient to cover potential damages. The worst realistic outcome if invoked is a significant financial loss that exceeds the liability limit. This clause is somewhat restrictive and may not be standard in all industry contracts.
clauses.key_text: 5.1 Limitation of Liability
steps: STEP 1: The parties' liability is limited to $50,000.
STEP 2: The worst realistic outcome if invoked is a significant financial loss that exceeds the liability limit.
STEP 3: This clause is somewhat restrictive and may not be standard in all industry contracts.
STEP 4: Risk score: 80 (high) due to the potential consequences of a significant financial loss.

clauses.title: Indemnification
clauses.risk_reason: The clause requires each party to indemnify the other party for breaches of the agreement. The worst realistic outcome if invoked is a significant financial loss due to indemnification obligations. This clause is standard in industry contracts, but the scope of indemnification may be considered somewhat broad.
clauses.key_text: 5.3 Indemnification
steps: STEP 1: Each party must indemnify the other party for breaches of the agreement.
STEP 2: The worst realistic outcome if invoked is a significant financial loss due to indemnification obligations.
STEP 3: This clause is standard in industry contracts, but the scope of indemnification may be considered somewhat broad.
STEP 4: Risk score: 70 (high) due to the potential consequences of indemnification obligations.

clauses.title: Payment Terms
clauses.risk_reason: The clause requires Party B to pay a one-time access fee of $25,000. The worst realistic outcome if invoked is a late payment or non-payment, which could lead to interest accrual. This clause is standard in industry contracts.
clauses.key_text: 6.1 Fees
steps: STEP 1: Party B must pay a one-time access fee of $25,000.
STEP 2: The worst realistic outcome if invoked is a late payment or non-payment, which could lead to interest accrual.
STEP 3: This clause is standard in industry contracts.
STEP 4: Risk score: 30 (low-medium) due to the relatively low risk of non-payment.

clauses.title: Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
clauses.risk_reason: The clause specifies that the agreement is governed by Delaware law and disputes will be resolved through arbitration. The worst realistic outcome if invoked is a dispute that requires arbitration, which could lead to significant legal fees. This clause is standard in industry contracts.
clauses.key_text: 7.1 Governing Law
steps: STEP 1: The agreement is governed by Delaware law.
STEP 2: The worst realistic outcome if invoked is a dispute that requires arbitration, which could lead to significant legal fees.
STEP 3: This clause is standard in industry contracts.
STEP 4: Risk score: 20 (low) due to the relatively low risk of disputes requiring arbitration.

clauses.title: Force Majeure
clauses.risk_reason: The clause excuses parties from performing their obligations due to force majeure events. The worst realistic outcome if invoked is a delay or failure to perform obligations, which could lead to minor financial losses. This clause is standard in industry contracts.
clauses.key_text: 8.1 Force Majeure Event
steps: STEP 1: The parties are excused from performing their obligations due to force majeure events.
STEP 2: The worst realistic outcome if invoked is a delay or failure to perform obligations, which could lead to minor financial losses.
STEP 3: This clause is standard in industry contracts.
STEP 4: Risk score: 10 (low) due to the relatively low risk of force majeure events.

clauses.title: Automatic Renewal
clauses.risk_reason: The clause provides for automatic renewal of the agreement unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal. The worst realistic outcome if invoked is an unintended renewal of the agreement, which could lead to financial losses. This clause is relatively standard in industry contracts.
clauses.key_text: 4.1 Term
steps: STEP 1: The agreement automatically renews unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal.
STEP 2: The worst realistic outcome if invoked is an unintended renewal of the agreement, which could lead to financial losses.
STEP 3: This clause is relatively standard in industry contracts, but the automatic renewal provision may be considered somewhat restrictive.
STEP 4: Risk score: 45 (medium) due to the potential consequences of unintended renewal.

clauses.title: Direct Damages Cap

clauses.key_text: shall not exceed United States Dollars Fifty Thousand (USD 50,000)

clauses.title: Non-Disclosure
clauses.risk_reason: The confidentiality obligations are strict and could lead to significant liability if breached.
clauses.key_text: Each Party agrees to: (a) hold all Confidential Information of the other Party in strict confidence; (b) not disclose to any third party without prior written consent; (c) use solely for the Business Purpose; and (d) protect using the same degree of care it uses for its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care.
steps: Each party must keep the other's Confidential Information confidential and only use it for the agreed purpose.
The worst outcome could be significant financial penalties or damages if a party breaches confidentiality.
This language is fairly standard in the industry, but the strictness of the obligations increases risk.

clauses.title: Termination for Convenience
clauses.risk_reason: Termination can be executed with minimal notice, which may disrupt business operations.
clauses.key_text: Either Party may terminate upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other Party.
steps: Either party can terminate the agreement with 30 days' notice.
The worst outcome could be sudden termination leading to loss of business opportunities or investments.
This is a common clause in the industry, allowing flexibility for both parties.

clauses.title: Ownership
clauses.risk_reason: Clear ownership of Confidential Information reduces potential disputes over IP rights.
clauses.key_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the sole and exclusive property of the Disclosing Party.
steps: The Disclosing Party retains ownership of all Confidential Information.
The worst outcome would be disputes over ownership if the clause were not clear.
This is standard language in the industry, ensuring clarity on IP ownership.

clauses.title: Limitation of Liability
clauses.risk_reason: Limiting liability can expose parties to greater risk in case of significant claims.
clauses.key_text: IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, PUNITIVE, OR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES.
steps: Neither party is liable for indirect or consequential damages.
The worst outcome could be substantial losses that cannot be recovered due to this limitation.
This limitation is common in the industry, but the extent of the exclusions raises risk.

clauses.title: Indemnification
clauses.risk_reason: Indemnification obligations can lead to significant financial exposure if breaches occur.
clauses.key_text: Each Party shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the other Party from all losses, damages, liabilities, claims, and expenses.
steps: Each party must indemnify the other for breaches of the agreement.
The worst outcome could be substantial legal costs and damages if a breach occurs.
Indemnification clauses are standard, but the broad scope increases risk.

clauses.title: Fees
clauses.risk_reason: Payment terms are straightforward and manageable.
clauses.key_text: Party B agrees to pay Party A a one-time access fee of United States Dollars Twenty-Five Thousand (USD 25,000).
steps: Party B must pay a specified fee within 30 days.
The worst outcome would be late payment penalties, which are manageable.
Payment terms are standard in the industry and clear.

clauses.title: Term
clauses.risk_reason: Automatic renewal can lead to unintended commitments if not monitored.
clauses.key_text: Upon expiration, this Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one (1) year periods unless either Party provides written notice of non-renewal.
steps: The agreement automatically renews unless one party opts out with notice.
The worst outcome could be being bound to an agreement longer than intended.
Auto-renewal clauses are common, but they require careful management to avoid risks.

clauses.title: Governing Law
clauses.risk_reason: Governing law is clearly defined, reducing ambiguity in legal proceedings.
clauses.key_text: This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of Delaware.
steps: The agreement is governed by Delaware law.
The worst outcome could be jurisdictional issues if not properly defined.
This is standard practice in contracts, providing clarity.

clauses.title: Force Majeure Event
clauses.risk_reason: Force majeure clauses can limit liability but may also lead to disputes over what constitutes a force majeure event.
clauses.key_text: Neither Party shall be liable for failure or delay caused by circumstances beyond reasonable control.
steps: Neither party is liable for delays caused by uncontrollable events.
The worst outcome could be disputes over whether an event qualifies as force majeure.
Force majeure clauses are standard, but their interpretation can vary.
